Artist Portrait

// 001 — About

Art is the
language
I speak

Hi, I'm the artist behind TheRKarts — a freelance creator passionate about the entire spectrum from pencil to pixel. I blend the rawness of hand-drawn illustration with the precision of digital editing to create work that feels both deeply human and technically refined.

Whether you need a custom portrait, a surreal digital composite, or photo retouching that tells a story, I bring the same care and intention to every piece. Each artwork is a collaboration between my vision and yours.

// 003 — Testimonials

What clients
say

"

RK captured something in my portrait that I didn't even know was there. The linework is hauntingly precise — it doesn't just look like me, it feels like me. I've commissioned three pieces now and each one leaves me speechless.

A
Ananya Krishnan
Portrait Commission
Drawing
"

I needed my brand photos completely transformed — moody, editorial, cinematic. TheRKarts delivered beyond anything I imagined. The retouching is seamless and the composite work is film-quality. My engagement doubled after the rebrand.

M
Mihir Shetty
Brand Photography Edit
Editing
"

The watercolour painting RK made for our wedding gift wall is the most treasured thing in our home. The warmth, the texture, the way light seems to glow from within it — guests always stop and stare. Pure talent and pure heart.

// 005 — Contact

Let's create
together

Have a project in mind? Whether it's a custom illustration, a painting commission, or Photoshop work — I'd love to hear from you. Let's talk about what you're envisioning.

Typical response time is within 24–48 hours. Looking forward to creating something special with you.

From the
Studio

Featured Cover Image
Feb 18, 2026 8 min read

How I Achieve Hyper-Realistic Eyes in Pencil — A Step-by-Step Breakdown

The eye is the most emotionally charged subject in portraiture. After years of studying anatomy, light behaviour, and graphite behaviour on different paper textures, I've developed a layered approach that creates depth and wet-glass realism. In this post I walk through every stage — from the initial light sketch to the final reflected highlight that brings the eye to life.
